refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect. The refined FR explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated), incorporates the safety and policy constraints as boundary conditions, and defines the measurable outcome (≥ target over evaluation period) as the verification hook.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 40 operating hours). Log task cycles, material moved (m³ or ton), elapsed time, and safety/policy compliance events. Calculate productivity = total_material / total_time. Compare against productivity_target. Verify zero safety plan violations and operational policy breaches during trial.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving task cycles (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency ≤ latency_threshold and situational awareness ≥ awareness_threshold.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe operational states.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report productivity metrics (material moved, cycle time, utilization) for post-trial analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance hydraulic excavator with optimized bucket geometry, boom kinematics, and operator controls to minimize cycle time; pair with tele-operated command interface and real-time safety monitoring to achieve target productivity within safety constraints.

DP-B: Deploy autonomous or semi-autonomous vehicle with AI-driven task planning and execution, supported by remote monitoring and safety override capability, to achieve consistent high-productivity cycles independent of operator skill variation.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid human-machine system where on-site operator executes routine cycles with tele-operator oversight and intervention capability; safety services continuously validate state against worksite plan; productivity metrics are logged and analyzed post-trial.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a hybrid human-machine system where on-site operator executes routine cycles with tele-operator oversight and intervention capability; safety services continuously validate state against worksite plan; productivity metrics are logged and analyzed post-trial.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle cycle time (DP-C vehicle component) and teleoperation latency (DP-C teleoperation component): if latency exceeds ~500 ms, tele-operator cannot intervene safely; mitigated by latency_threshold specification.
Coupling between safety services validation (DP-C safety component) and operator command execution: safety constraints may delay or block commands; acceptable as safety is high criticality.

Coupling between productivity measurement (DP-C metrics component) and operational variability: different operators, site conditions, material types will produce variance; mitigated by representative operations trial design.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is a system-level design that distributes responsibility across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. Couplings are expected and managed via interface specifications (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert). No hidden or unmanaged couplings identified.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: On-site operator is trained and competent; tele-operator can intervene within latency_threshold.
ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan is well-defined and can be encoded into safety services logic.
ASSUMPTION: Network link (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) is reliable with latency ≤ latency_threshold.
RISK: If on-site operator skill is low, cycle time may exceed target even with optimized vehicle; mitigation: operator training program.
RISK: If safety services constraints are overly restrictive, they may artificially limit productivity; mitigation: safety plan validation and tuning during trial.
RISK: If network latency spikes, tele-operator cannot intervene; mitigation: fallback to on-site operator autonomous mode with reduced productivity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: None at this level. DP-C is well-justified for a high-safety-criticality system with dual operator modes. Proceed to IM assignment and sub-FR decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the overall productivity delivery logic, metrics aggregation, and system-level safety state machine. External interfaces: Tasking (in), Telemetry (out), SafetyAlert (inout), Network Link (inout).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands from on-site operator (via Vehicle HMI) or tele-operator (via Teleoperation interface). Format: task_id, task_type (dig, load, transport, dump), target_location, priority, safety_constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state (position, orientation, bucket load, cycle phase), safety state (alerts, violations, override status), and productivity metrics (cycle_count, material_moved, elapsed_time) to monitoring systems and post-trial analysis.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional safety communication. Inbound: safety plan constraints, worksite hazard updates, emergency stop signals. Outbound: safety violations, intervention requests, override confirmations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying communication channel for Tasking, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert. Carries latency and reliability characteristics critical to tele-operator intervention capability.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for DP-C. It owns the hybrid human-machine orchestration, safety state machine, and metrics aggregation. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link infrastructure is external.

Vehicle module owns the physical excavator and on-site operator HMI, but does not own teleoperation, safety services, or metrics aggregation. Assigning DP-C to Vehicle would scatter teleoperation and safety logic across multiple modules, violating cohesion.

Teleoperation module owns remote command interface and latency management, but does not own vehicle control, safety services, or on-site operator integration. Assigning DP-C to Teleoperation would exclude on-site operator mode and safety services, breaking the hybrid design.

Safety Services module owns safety plan enforcement and constraint validation, but does not own vehicle, teleoperation, or productivity metrics. Assigning DP-C to Safety Services would invert the architecture: safety would become the orchestrator rather than a constraint enforcer.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it is the only module that naturally contains the hybrid human-machine orchestration logic, the safety state machine that coordinates vehicle and teleoperation, and the metrics aggregation that measures productivity. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that NGHE Solution integrates via Tasking, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert interfaces.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a 'god module' that owns too much logic, it may become difficult to test and maintain. MITIGATION: Decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (e.g., TaskOrchestrator, SafetyStateMachine, MetricsAggregator) at the next decomposition level.

RISK: If the boundary between NGHE Solution and Vehicle is unclear, ownership of on-site operator HMI and vehicle control may be ambiguous. MITIGATION: Clarify that Vehicle owns the physical excavator and on-site HMI; NGHE Solution owns the task orchestration logic that interprets on-site operator input and coordinates with teleoperation and safety services.

RISK: If Network Link is external to NGHE Solution, latency and reliability issues may not be owned by any module. MITIGATION: Assign Network Link ownership to a separate Infrastructure module or explicitly allocate latency/reliability requirements to NGHE Solution as a constraint.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the ownership boundary between NGHE Solution and Vehicle: NGHE Solution owns task orchestration and safety state machine; Vehicle owns physical excavator, on-site HMI, and cycle execution. Define sub-modules of NGHE Solution (TaskOrchestrator, SafetyStateMachine, MetricsAggregator) at the next decomposition level to reduce god-module risk.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-001 clearly specifies the productivity target, dual operator modes, safety constraints, and evaluation period. DP-C (hybrid human-machine system) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR: it enables on-site and tele-operated task execution, enforces safety constraints via safety services, and logs metrics for post-trial analysis. No gaps or misalignments detected.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: DP-C's hybrid orchestration logic is concentrated in NGHE Solution (95% containment). If NGHE Solution is not further decomposed, the orchestration logic may become monolithic. MITIGATION: Plan sub-module decomposition (TaskOrchestrator, SafetyStateMachine, MetricsAggregator) at next level.

DIFFUSION RISK: Network Link is external to NGHE Solution. If latency or reliability issues arise, ownership and accountability may be unclear. MITIGATION: Assign Network Link to Infrastructure module or explicitly allocate latency/reliability SLAs to NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary with 95% containment score. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules integrated via Tasking, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert interfaces. Ownership is clear: NGHE Solution owns orchestration; sub-modules own their respective domains.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of refined FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trial: log task cycles, material moved, cycle time, and safety/policy compliance. Calculate productivity = total_material / total_time. Compare against productivity_target. NGHE Solution owns metrics aggregation and logging, so verification data is directly traceable to IM boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of refined FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively. Each sub-FR has a clear owner and verification method.

recommended_revisions: Decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (TaskOrchestrator, SafetyStateMachine, MetricsAggregator) at the next level to reduce god-module risk and improve testability.
Clarify ownership of Network Link: assign to Infrastructure module or allocate latency/reliability SLAs explicitly to NGHE Solution.
Define latency_threshold, awareness_threshold, and cycle_time_target as quantitative acceptance criteria for sub-FRs.

decision.justification: Refined FR-NGHE-001 is non-atomic and has been decomposed into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4). DP-C is well-justified and passes FR-DP coupling check. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary with 95% containment. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. Proceed to decompose each sub-FR at the next level to allocate responsibility to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution sub-modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving task cycles (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency ≤ latency_threshold and situational awareness ≥ awareness_threshold.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe operational states.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report productivity metrics (material moved, cycle time, utilization) for post-trial analysis.

assumptions: On-site operator is trained and competent; tele-operator can intervene within latency_threshold.
Worksite safety plan is well-defined and can be encoded into safety services logic.
Network link (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) is reliable with latency ≤ latency_threshold.
Representative operations trial is conducted over agreed period (e.g., 40 operating hours) with consistent site conditions and material type.
Productivity target is achievable with optimized vehicle, trained operators, and effective safety services.
open_risks: If on-site operator skill is low, cycle time may exceed target even with optimized vehicle.
If safety services constraints are overly restrictive, they may artificially limit productivity.
If network latency spikes, tele-operator cannot intervene; fallback to on-site operator autonomous mode may reduce productivity.
If NGHE Solution becomes a god module, it may become difficult to test and maintain.
If Network Link ownership is unclear, latency and reliability issues may not be owned by any module.
evidence_needed: Quantitative acceptance criteria: productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h), latency_threshold (ms), awareness_threshold (%), cycle_time_target (s).
Worksite safety plan specification: hazards, constraints, emergency procedures.
Network link SLA: latency, bandwidth, reliability, failover behavior.
Operator training program and competency assessment criteria.
Representative operations trial protocol: duration, site conditions, material type, logging requirements.
